tr.td: Acute non-ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units (0.1 mL) subcutaneously before breakfast and before dinner
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (6.25 mg) by mouth twice daily
1 tablet (81 mg) by mouth once daily
Atorvastatin (as atorvastatin calcium) 40 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (100 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms per spray, repeat every 5 minutes as needed for chest pain, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment of health and social care needs
Acute non-ST segment elevation myocardial infarction (disorder)
Assessment of cardiac status using monitoring device

ul.li: Achieve and maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve and maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will implement and maintain a diabetes self-management plan to delay or prevent progression to type 2 diabetes.
Maintain hemoglobin A1c below 6.5% to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
Maintain fasting plasma glucose below 7.0 mmol/L to prevent progression to diabetes.
Achieve and maintain at least 5% weight loss from baseline to improve glycemic control and reduce diabetes risk.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.

tr.td: Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)
Weight difference [Mass difference] --pre dialysis - post dialysis
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Glucose [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip
pH of Urine by Test strip

Protein [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

ul.li: Achieve and maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve and maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will implement and maintain a diabetes self-management plan to delay or prevent progression to type 2 diabetes.
